text: G'morning. I'm SonicBlu Coyote, and I'd like to talk to you for a few minutes about your YTPs. I must say your brilliant YTPs have done more than entertain us, but have also opened up a whole new perspective onto things we never thought we'd see. For instance, I never knew Wilford Brimley could sing....but I found out via this YTP video. Now, I'm not perfect, and I know I've slipped up, and I've done things I shouldn't do, and I've failed to like videos and subscribe, but I'm gonna tell you. When I don't slip up and I DO subscribe, and I DO like and share videos and I TAKE my medicine and I DOOOO the things I'm supposed to DOOO, I DOOO subscribe to those who deserve it. I'm surrounded by many YTPers. And along the trail, I've seen a lot of YTPers work their magic, but yours always seem to rise to the top. I was very very fortunate to stumble upon your videos so long ago. And in doing so, I've come to look forward to your latest YTP videos which adds to the myriad of videos you've done before. There's a line to a song, "Just believe me, don't watch." but if I don't watch, I can't laugh all my cares away. I'd like to say, I will, I will subscribe to you and like your videos, the comparison is unreal. In closing, I would like to simply say to you, keep up the great work. text:
Two decades of YTP...
This makes me glad I composed a little orchestral piece way back in university, like 14 or 15 years ago, in honour of the genre. Because YTP is art goddammit: it's inane, it's ridiculous, it's got POOP in its friggin' name for crying out loud! But IT IS ART, and one of the most accessible kinds of art for us Millennials, Gens Y, Z, and Alpha - and hell, there's probably some Gen Xers that get a kick outta the HOH SIS goodness!
